data.title: [tickets branch sync]: concurrent session orphan events -> push rebase conflict
data.description:
## Incident Overview
During end-session push of tickets branch comments, push attempts repeatedly failed with 'rebase conflict (attempt 1)' for 11+ ticket comment commits. Diagnosis revealed 4 untracked event JSON files for ticket 65ff-7657 (a task from a concurrent session, created at timestamp 1776643578). The concurrent session created events but never committed/pushed them; my sessions local tickets branch had 11 commits ready to push; pull --rebase aborted with 'could not detach HEAD' because the untracked files would be overwritten by the rebase.

## Expected Behavior
(a) Ticket events from concurrent sessions should be auto-committed to the local tickets branch at create time so no loose files are left uncommitted, OR (b) the tickets branch push/rebase helper should detect and handle concurrent session orphan events by staging and committing them before rebase, OR (c) the ticket-lib write path should hold a sync lock across event-write plus commit to prevent races.

## Actual Behavior
The tickets tracker directory contains 4 uncommitted event JSONs for a ticket belonging to another session. Push cannot rebase on top of these because rebase requires a clean working tree. Root cause: no session-level atomicity between 'create event file' and 'commit plus push'. Concurrent sessions leave orphan uncommitted files that block push from any other session that runs afterward.

## Reproduction
1. Session A: run ticket create/comment/link/transition operations; crash or disconnect before push completes
2. Session B: any ticket mutation plus push to tickets branch
3. Observe: push fails with rebase conflict because Session A's uncommitted events are still in the working tree
